judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all three expectations with essentially equivalent substance; Report 2 is somewhat more thorough (exact line citations, notes zero test coverage) but both meet the graded criteria comparably.

report_1.evidence: `search_appointments(session, tenant_id, q, status)` accepts `tenant_id` as a parameter but never uses it. ... There is no `tenant_id = :tenant_id` clause anywhere. Every other read path in this codebase goes through `build_appointment_query`, which is the single place tenant scoping is enforced

report_2.evidence: `tenant_id` is accepted as a parameter (line 10) but is **never referenced** in the SQL — there is no `AND tenant_id = ...` clause anywhere. Every other read path in this codebase is built on `build_appointment_query` (`backend/app/db.py:7-10`), which does apply `Appointment.tenant_id == tenant_id`

report_1.evidence: signature changed from `(session, tenant_id)` to `(session, tenant_id, filters)`, with **no default value** for `filters`. Call site: `backend/app/exports.py:9`, `nightly_export`, which still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` with only two positional arguments — this file is untouched by the PR

report_2.evidence: File: `backend/app/exports.py`, line 9 — this file is **not touched by the PR** ... and still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` with only two positional arguments. On the PR branch, any invocation of `nightly_export` ... will raise `TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'`

report_1.evidence: Because the endpoint parameter is typed `AppointmentStatus` — a `str, Enum` with fixed members `booked/cancelled/completed` — FastAPI's request validation rejects any value outside those three before the function body runs, so this isn't exploitable through the HTTP route today.

report_2.evidence: This is **not currently exploitable through the HTTP endpoint** — `status` is typed `AppointmentStatus`, a `str, Enum` (`backend/app/schemas.py:5-8`), and FastAPI rejects any query value outside `booked`/`cancelled`/`completed` with a 422 before the function is ever called.
